evt_014.label: Drop
evt_014.basis: Company F exited after saying it wanted only limited, select assets rather than a whole-company transaction.
evt_014.source_text: Company F declined the invitation to schedule a management presentation, indicating it was only interested in purchasing limited, select assets of sTec

evt_015.label: Drop
evt_015.basis: Company E dropped out after saying it was interested only in limited, select assets.
evt_015.source_text: Company E indicated it was also only interested in purchasing limited, select assets of sTec

evt_018.label: Drop
evt_018.basis: Company G simply said it would not continue in the process.
evt_018.source_text: Company G indicated it would not continue in the process

evt_028.label: Drop
evt_028.basis: WDC stepped back from the process and said it was not prepared to move forward with a transaction at that time.
evt_028.source_text: WDC had decided to reconsider its interest in acquiring sTec and that it was not prepared to move forward with a transaction with sTec at that time.

evt_029.label: Drop
evt_029.basis: Company D disengaged because it could not continue due diligence for more than two weeks.
evt_029.source_text: Company D would not be in a position to actively conduct due diligence for more than two weeks and was disengaging from the process

initiation_judgment.basis: The board launched the sale process on its own by authorizing strategic-alternatives outreach before any bidder or activist pressure is described as triggering the process.
initiation_judgment.source_text: Consequently, in mid-November, 2012, our board of directors authorized management to contact potential financial advisors to explore their interest in representing the company in its exploration of strategic alternatives, including a potential sale of the company.

advisor_gibson_dunn.source_text: Gibson, Dunn & Crutcher LLP ( Gibson Dunn), which had been engaged in October 2012 as the companys outside corporate counsel

advisor_bofa_merrill_lynch.source_text: the board approved retaining BofA Merrill Lynch to act as sTecs financial advisor

advisor_mackenzie_partners.source_text: representatives from MacKenzie Partners, sTecs proxy solicitor

advisor_latham_watkins.source_text: Latham & Watkins LLP ( Latham & Watkins), litigation counsel to sTec and Mr. Manouch Moshayedi

advisor_paul_hastings.source_text: Paul Hastings LLP ( Paul Hastings), counsel to Messrs. Moshayedi

advisor_shearman_sterling.source_text: Shearman & Sterling LLP ( Shearman), counsel to WDC

advisor_company_a_financial_advisor.source_text: an investment bank representing Company A

count_reconciliation.assertion: investment banking firms identified by the board of directors
count_reconciliation.note: Only BofA Merrill Lynch is individually extracted; the other board-identified firms were counted in aggregate and not named in the filing narrative.

count_reconciliation.assertion: prospective acquirers contacted by BofA Merrill Lynch
count_reconciliation.note: Nine April 2013 outreach counterparties are individually represented in the extract, while the filing counts nine additional prospective acquirers only in aggregate.
Eight technology-company counterparties are individually represented in the extract; the filing counts nine additional technology companies only in aggregate.

count_reconciliation.assertion: strategic acquirers interested in limited, select assets
count_reconciliation.note: Company C, Company E, and Company F are individually represented, while three additional limited-asset expressions of interest are only counted in aggregate in the filing.

count_reconciliation.assertion: prospective acquirers not interested in a potential transaction
count_reconciliation.note: Only Company A and the unnamed financial sponsor are individually represented among the no-interest responses; the remaining seven were not named separately in the filing.

count_reconciliation.assertion: executed non-disclosure agreements related to exploration of a potential sale
